page: Amazon Web Services r6in.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes support for advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with top-tier results in Passmark CPU encryption, extended instructions, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering strong read and write speeds for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks. The instance lacks local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but compensates with a substantial 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth to support high-throughput EBS volumes and cluster communication.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6in.32xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, minimizing the need to scale horizontally solely for RAM capacity. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, enterprise database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While it lacks local storage, its architectural design is optimized for network-attached EBS performance. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in CPU encryption, extended instructions, and ray tracing, alongside strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads without requiring local disk storage. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale enterprise database operations, and high-throughput data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for large-scale databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 instance featuring one terabyte of RAM, high-speed network bandwidth, and dedicated Intel Xeon processing power.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory for memory-intensive applications
Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz with AVX-512 support
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and cluster networking
Top-tier benchmark performance in CPU encryption and multi-core operations
Designed for in-memory databases, enterprise workloads, and data processing
